mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and signals policy accommodation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as risk premia compress.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings weakness, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) are contracting while the federal funds rate remains elevated or slow to fall relative to the contraction pace, the joint signal indicates credit-constrained housing demand destruction that propagates to broader equity risk sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month decline exceeds 10% while FEDFUNDS decline lags HOUST decline pace
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-FEDFUNDS signal fails to outperform a HOUST-only univariate model by more than 0.02 Rank IC over any 3-year window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary regime where FEDFUNDS is near the zero lower bound, a continued decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals ongoing real-economy contraction that outweighs monetary stimulus, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; outside this regime the same INDPRO decline is less predictive because monetary policy still has room to offset it.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS < 2%
failure_condition: During low-FEDFUNDS regimes, INDPRO declines are not associated with significantly negative forward returns relative to the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two recessionary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer spending and near-term corporate revenue strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice near-term growth expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5d forward returns is below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, indicating no short-horizon predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month uptrend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real output capacity and corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following sustained INDPRO uptrends are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05) across at least three distinct business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 300,000 jobs signals an abrupt labor market shock that triggers risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, SPX 20d forward returns are positive despite the payroll shock, or Rank IC is below 0.03 across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) is falling while the federal funds rate remains sticky above the pace of disinflation, the rising real interest rate compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; the effect requires both series moving jointly since neither alone captures the real-rate channel.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month decline while FEDFUNDS unchanged or rising
failure_condition: The joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS real-rate signal fails to beat a FEDFUNDS-only baseline by more than 0.02 Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no true interaction effect.
